title: Tomo Instructions
description: Canonical, machine-readable instruction set produced by instruction-render.py. Consumed by Tomo Hashi (Obsidian plugin) for execution and rendered to a human-readable markdown view.

source_suggestions.description: Filename (vault-relative or stem) of the suggestions doc this set was derived from.

tomo_version.description: Tomo version that produced this file.

md_peer.description: Stem of the companion human-readable markdown, in the same folder (e.g. '2026-04-21_0918_instructions'). Explicit linkage rather than inferred from timestamp/convention — lets Tomo Hashi refuse with a clear error when the peer is missing or renamed. Always populated by instruction-render.py v0.6.0+; not in the required set so v0.5.x artefacts still validate.

action_id.description: Monotonic action identifier (I01, I02, …). Unique within the instruction set.

applied_field.description: True when Tomo Hashi has successfully executed this action. Defaults to false on emission. Monotonic — Hashi only writes false → true; Tomo never re-emits true. Optional for v0.5.x backwards-compat (missing = treated as false).

source.description: Vault-relative full path of the rendered file currently in the inbox (what gets moved), e.g. '100 Inbox/2026-04-21_1200_asahikawa-....md'.

destination.description: Vault-relative full target path including the final filename, e.g. 'Atlas/202 Notes/Asahikawa — ....md'.

title.description: Final title of the note (also the stem of the destination filename).

rendered_file.description: Basename of the rendered file (redundant with source; kept for display).

origin_inbox_item.description: Optional vault-relative path of the original inbox item this note was derived from. The delete_source action is the operational signal for cleanup; by default the renderer emits a paired delete_source for this origin unless the user opts out via the suggestions doc's 'Keep origin' checkbox.

target_moc_path.description: Resolved vault-relative path of the MOC, if known.

anchor.description: Where in the target MOC to find the insertion point. type=callout matches the callout opening line; type=heading matches a heading by text; type=line matches a body line by content.

placement.description: Where to write relative to the matched anchor. inside (callout only) appends as last line of callout body with > prefix. after inserts immediately after the matched anchor's terminal line, verbatim.

line_to_add.description: Pre-formatted line content to insert (bullet style, link form, etc. all decided Tomo-side). Hashi writes this verbatim, prepending '> ' only when placement=inside on a callout anchor.

type.description: callout = match callout opening line (e.g. '[!blocks] Key Concepts'); heading = match heading text without # prefix (any heading level); line = match body line by literal content.

value.description: Content of the anchor to match. Null is permitted only at emission time when the renderer cannot resolve a concrete value yet (resolver populates later via Kado read).

target_moc.description: MOC stem for human-readable display (no path, no .md).

target_moc_path.description: Resolved vault-relative path of the target MOC.

marker.description: Dataview inline-field marker that locates the line to replace, e.g. 'up::' or 'related::'. Hashi locates the first line whose stripped content (after optional '> ' callout prefix) starts with this marker.

line.description: Pre-formatted full line content to write, e.g. 'up:: [[Hobbies (MOC)]]' or 'related:: [[A]], [[B]]'. Hashi replaces the located marker line with this verbatim, preserving the line's leading '> ' prefix if the marker was inside a callout. Multi-link aggregation for related:: is done Tomo-side.

section.description: Daily log heading (e.g. 'Daily Log'), without leading #.

source.description: Vault-relative full path of the rendered MOC file currently in the inbox, e.g. '100 Inbox/2026-04-21_1200_brettspiele-moc.md'.

destination.description: Vault-relative full target path of the MOC, e.g. 'Atlas/200 Maps/Brettspiele (MOC).md'.

rendered_file.description: Basename of the rendered MOC file (redundant with source; kept for display).

parent_moc.description: Parent MOC stem for up-linking (emitted separately as an add_relationship action with marker='up::' on this MOC).

supporting_items.description: Raw 'Supporting items' field from the Proposed MOC block (e.g. 'S02, S06, S12'). Consumed by the renderer to generate link_to_moc actions for each supporting item into this new MOC.
